description: Fix language-less code blocks receiving inline code styling (background, border, padding) from the MDX code component override. The code override in MDX.tsx applies inline-code styles to ALL code elements, including code inside pre (language-less code blocks). This causes visual misalignment and incorrect styling in output example blocks.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Skip inline code styling for code elements inside language-less code blocks
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want language-less code blocks to render with consistent code-block styling (not inline-code styling) so that output examples like log lines are properly aligned without extra padding, borders, or mismatched backgrounds.
acceptanceCriteria: The code component override in docs/components/MDX.tsx detects when it is rendering a code block (not inline code) and skips inline-code styling in that case

Detection uses two signals: (1) presence of data-language or data-theme attributes means rehype-pretty-code processed it as a block — skip inline styling, and (2) if children is a string containing a newline character, it is a language-less code block — skip inline styling
When the code override detects block context, it renders a plain code element with only the className passed through (no bg-inline-code-bg, no text-inline-code-fg, no border, no px-1.5 py-0.5)
Inline code (backtick text in prose) continues to receive the full inline-code styling: bg-inline-code-bg, text-inline-code-fg, border, px-1.5, py-0.5, font-mono, text-sm

The language-less code block in docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx (lines 188-191, the [plugin:slack] log example) renders without a visible border, without inline-code background, and with both lines left-aligned at the same position
The language-less code block in docs/content/docs/guides/error-handling.mdx (line 13, Tool dispatch error) renders without inline-code styling
Code blocks with a language (```bash, ```typescript, etc.) are unaffected — rehype-pretty-code overrides their styling via inline style attributes regardless
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
